questions.question: If the code for HAND is IBOE (each letter moved forward by 1), what is the code for FEET?

questions.question: If the code for FROG is HTQI (each letter moved forward by 2), what is the code for TOAD?

questions.question: If the code for LAMP is ODPS, what is the code for DARK?

questions.question: If PARK is coded as MXOH, what word is coded as JXII?
questions.explanation: Each letter in the code has been moved back 3. To decode, move forward 3: J→M, X→A, I→L, I→L = MALL. Tip: Think of the alphabet as a circle — after Z comes A again, and before A comes Z. Count carefully at the boundaries! ✓

questions.question: If the code for HERO is LIVS, what is the code for KING?

questions.question: If the code for TIGER is PECAN, what is the code for HORSE?

questions.question: If the code for CAMP is IGSV, what is the code for TENT?
questions.explanation: Each letter shifts forward by 6: T→Z, E→K, N→T, T→Z. So TENT becomes ZKTZ. Tip: When the shift changes each position, write the pattern above the letters. Look for +1,+2,+3,+4 or similar sequences. ✓

questions.question: If SMILE is coded as WQMPI, what word is coded as ASVPH?
questions.explanation: Each letter has been moved forward 4. To decode, move back 4: A→W, S→O, V→R, P→L, H→D = WORLD. Tip: Always work out the rule from the example first, then apply it letter by letter to the new word. ✓

questions.question: If BIRD is coded as CKSF (1st letter +1, 2nd +2, 3rd +1, 4th +2), what is the code for FROG?
questions.explanation: The pattern alternates +1, +2: F→G(+1), R→T(+2), O→P(+1), G→I(+2). So FROG becomes GTPI. Tip: +1 is the most common GL code — practise it until it's automatic! ✓

questions.question: If HELP is coded as SVOK using the reverse alphabet (A=Z, B=Y, C=X...), what is the code for FIRE?
questions.explanation: Using reverse alphabet: F→U, I→R, R→I, E→V. So FIRE becomes URIV. Tip: Always work out the rule from the example first, then apply it letter by letter to the new word. ✓

questions.question: If CROWN is coded as FURZQ, what word is coded as VWDUW?
questions.explanation: Each letter has been moved forward 3. To decode, move back 3: V→S, W→T, D→A, U→R, W→T = START. Tip: Always work out the rule from the example first, then apply it letter by letter to the new word. ✓

questions.question: If HAPPY is coded as ICSQA (1st +1, 2nd +2, 3rd +3, 4th +1, 5th +2), what is the code for SMILE?
questions.explanation: Following the pattern +1,+2,+3,+1,+2: S→T(+1), M→O(+2), I→L(+3), L→M(+1), E→G(+2). So SMILE becomes TOLMG. Tip: +1 is the most common GL code — practise it until it's automatic! ✓

questions.question: If DRAW is coded as ETDA, what is the code for FISH?
questions.explanation: Apply shifts +1, +2, +3, +4: F+1=G, I+2=K, S+3=V, H+4=L = GKVL. Tip: When the shift changes each position, write the pattern above the letters. Look for +1,+2,+3,+4 or similar sequences. ✓

questions.question: If BARK is coded as AYOG, what is the code for HELP?
questions.explanation: Apply shifts -1, -2, -3, -4: H-1=G, E-2=C, L-3=I, P-4=L = GCIL. Tip: When the shift changes each position, write the pattern above the letters. Look for +1,+2,+3,+4 or similar sequences. ✓

questions.question: If GAME is coded as HZND, what is the code for BOOK?

questions.question: If COLD is coded as DQOH, what is the code for MILK?
questions.explanation: Apply shifts +1, +2, +3, +4: M+1=N, I+2=K, L+3=O, K+4=O = NKOO. Tip: When the shift changes each position, write the pattern above the letters. Look for +1,+2,+3,+4 or similar sequences. ✓

questions.question: If HORSE is coded as KQSVG, what is the code for BEACH?

questions.question: If HELP is coded as KHOS, what is the code for YARD?
questions.explanation: Apply +3: Y+3=B, A+3=D, R+3=U, D+3=G = BDUG. Wrap-around! Each letter in the word shifts by +3 positions in the alphabet. Tip: For bigger shifts like +3, use the EJOTY trick (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) as anchor points. ✓

questions.question: If HELP is coded as JGNR, what is the code for WAXY?
questions.explanation: Apply +2: W+2=Y, A+2=C, X+2=Z, Y+2=A = YCZA. Wrap-around! Each letter in the word shifts by +2 positions in the alphabet. Tip: For +2, skip one letter each time. B+2=D, skipping C. ✓

questions.question: If HELP is coded as LIPT, what is the code for ZERO?
questions.explanation: Apply +4: Z+4=D, E+4=I, R+4=V, O+4=S = DIVS. Wrap-around! Each letter in the word shifts by +4 positions in the alphabet. Tip: Larger shifts are trickier — write the alphabet out and count carefully. Don't rush! ✓

questions.question: If HELP is coded as LIPT, what is the code for FIZZY?

questions.question: If STOP is coded as HGLK, what is the code for HELP?
questions.explanation: Mirror: H=S, E=V, L=O, P=K = SVOK. In mirror codes, each letter maps to its opposite in the alphabet — A↔Z, B↔Y, C↔X and so on. The brilliant thing is: to decode, you apply exactly the same mirror! Tip: In mirror codes, A=Z, B=Y, C=X and so on. The trick is that encoding and decoding use exactly the same rule! ✓

questions.question: If STOP is coded as HGLK, what is the code for COLD?
questions.explanation: Mirror: C=X, O=L, L=O, D=W = XLOW. In mirror codes, each letter maps to its opposite in the alphabet — A↔Z, B↔Y, C↔X and so on. The brilliant thing is: to decode, you apply exactly the same mirror! Tip: In mirror codes, A=Z, B=Y, C=X and so on. The trick is that encoding and decoding use exactly the same rule! ✓

questions.explanation: Mirror reverses itself: I=R, Z=A, R=I, M=N = RAIN. In mirror codes, each letter maps to its opposite in the alphabet — A↔Z, B↔Y, C↔X and so on. The brilliant thing is: to decode, you apply exactly the same mirror! Tip: In mirror codes, A=Z, B=Y, C=X and so on. The trick is that encoding and decoding use exactly the same rule! ✓

The brilliant thing is: to decode, you apply exactly the same mirror! Tip: In mirror codes, A=Z, B=Y, C=X and so on. The trick is that encoding and decoding use exactly the same rule! ✓

questions.question: If BLUE is coded as EOXH, what is the code for GOLD?

questions.explanation: Apply +4: D+4=H, E+4=I, S+4=W, K+4=O = HIWO. Each letter in the word shifts by +4 positions in the alphabet. Tip: Larger shifts are trickier — write the alphabet out and count carefully. Don't rush! ✓

questions.question: If BLUE is coded as XHQA, what is the code for JUMP?

questions.question: If BLUE is coded as EOXH, what is the code for TRAIN?

questions.question: If BLUE is coded as FPYI, what is the code for RIVER?
